controls.label: Does your business have a Customer Due Diligence — Partnerships process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Customer Due Diligence — Partnerships process in place. You must establish a documented Customer Due Diligence — Partnerships process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Have you implemented risk-based systems and controls to verify that the partnership exists and that all partner names have been collected?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based verification systems and controls
controls.remediation: Your Customer Due Diligence Program for Partnerships does not include the required risk-based systems and controls to verify partnership existence and confirm all partner names have been collected. Implement controls that enable you to be reasonably satisfied about partnership existence and partner identification, as required under rules 4.5.2, 4.5.2(1), and 4.5.2(2).

controls.label: Do you collect the full name of the partnership?
controls.remediation: Your Collection of KYC Information procedure does not collect the full name of the partnership. Update your process to collect the full name of every partnership customer before commencing a designated service, as required under rule 4.5.3(1).

controls.label: Do you collect the full business name of the partnership as registered under State or Territory business names legislation (if any)?
controls.remediation: Your Collection of KYC Information procedure does not collect the registered business name of the partnership under State or Territory legislation. Update your process to collect this information for every partnership customer, as required under rule 4.5.3(2).

controls.label: Do you collect the country in which the partnership was established?
controls.remediation: Your Collection of KYC Information procedure does not collect the country in which the partnership was established. Update your process to collect this information for every partnership customer, as required under rule 4.5.3(3).

controls.label: Do you collect the individual identification information required by your AML/CTF program for at least one partner in the partnership?
controls.detail-label: Describe what individual identification information you collect for partners
controls.remediation: Your Collection of KYC Information procedure does not collect individual identification information for at least one partner. Update your process to collect full name, date of birth, residential address, and identification details for at least one partner in the partnership, as required under rule 4.5.3(4).

controls.label: Do you collect the full name and residential address of each partner in the partnership (unless the partnership's regulated status is confirmed through a professional association directory)?
controls.detail-label: Describe how you collect partner names and addresses, including any exemptions you apply for professional associations
controls.remediation: Your Collection of KYC Information procedure does not collect the full name and residential address of each partner, or does not account for the professional association directory exemption. Update your process to collect this information for all partners unless the partnership's regulated status is confirmed through a current professional association membership directory, as required under rule 4.5.3(5).

items.label: Full name and residential address of each partner
Regulated status via professional association directory

controls.label: Have you implemented risk-based systems and controls to determine whether additional KYC information beyond the minimum should be collected?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk assessment process for determining additional collection requirements
controls.remediation: Your Customer Due Diligence Program for Partnerships does not include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether additional KYC information should be collected beyond the minimum. Implement a risk assessment procedure that evaluates each partnership and determines what additional information is necessary based on your ML/TF risk assessment, as required under rule 4.5.4.

controls.label: Do you verify the full name of the partnership using partnership agreements, certified copies of agreements, reliable independent documents, or reliable independent electronic data?
controls.detail-label: Describe the verification sources and methods you use for partnership names
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not verify the partnership name using acceptable sources. Update your process to verify the full partnership name from partnership agreements, certified copies of agreements, reliable independent documents, or reliable independent electronic data, as required under rule 4.5.5(1).

controls.label: Do you verify individual identification information for at least one partner in accordance with your applicable customer identification procedure for individuals?
controls.detail-label: Describe how you verify individual partner identity information
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not verify individual identification information for at least one partner. Update your process to verify full name, date of birth, and residential address for at least one partner using reliable and independent sources, as required under rule 4.5.5(2).

controls.label: Have you implemented risk-based systems and controls to determine whether, and to what extent, additional collected KYC information should be verified?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based procedure for determining additional verification requirements
controls.remediation: Your Customer Due Diligence Program for Partnerships does not include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether additional collected information should be verified. Implement a risk assessment procedure that determines the extent of verification required for additional KYC information based on your ML/TF risk assessment, as required under rule 4.5.6.

controls.label: Do you base partnership verification on partnership agreements, certified copies, certified extracts, reliable independent documents, reliable independent electronic data, or a combination of these?
controls.detail-label: Describe the specific sources and methods you use for partnership verification
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not use acceptable methods for partnership verification. Update your process to verify partnership information using partnership agreements, certified copies or extracts of agreements, partnership meeting minutes, reliable independent documents, reliable independent electronic data, or a combination of these sources, as required under rule 4.5.7.

controls.label: Do you accept disclosure certificates as verification evidence for partnership information when the information is not otherwise reasonably available from primary sources?
controls.detail-label: Describe how you accept and process disclosure certificates for partnership verification
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not account for disclosure certificates as an alternative verification method. Update your process to accept disclosure certificates when verifying partnership information for the purposes of determining additional collection requirements and when the information is not reasonably available from partnership agreements or other primary sources, as required under rules 4.5.8(1) and 4.5.8(2).

controls.label: Have you implemented risk-based systems and controls to respond to any discrepancies identified during partnership information verification?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for identifying, investigating, and resolving verification discrepancies
controls.remediation: Your Customer Due Diligence Program for Partnerships does not include risk-based systems and controls to respond to discrepancies in partnership verification. Implement a procedure that identifies and investigates discrepancies in partnership name, partner identity, or other information and determines whether you can remain reasonably satisfied about partnership existence and partner identification, as required under rule 4.5.9.

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: General CDD Obligation for Partnerships
groups.description: Risk-based systems and controls to verify partnership existence and confirm all partner names have been collected

groups.title: Collection of KYC Information
groups.description: Minimum KYC information to be collected about partnership customers

groups.title: Verification of Information
groups.description: Minimum verification procedures for partnership name and individual partner identification

groups.title: Verification Methods
groups.description: Acceptable sources and methods for verifying partnership information

groups.title: Additional KYC Assessment
groups.description: Risk-based determination of additional collection and verification requirements beyond the minimum

groups.title: Safe Harbour — Disclosure Certificates
groups.description: Acceptance and use of disclosure certificates as an alternative verification method when primary sources are not reasonably available

groups.title: Responding to Discrepancies
groups.description: Risk-based systems and controls to respond to discrepancies identified during partnership verification
